compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
Observed twice during epic cold-stall-chalk landing (blocked CI on #481 and #482):
- CI job "Run the integration tier" on **macos-latest / py3.13 ONLY** (ubuntu 3.11/3.12/3.13 all passed).
- Test: `tests/interfaces/io/test_import_batch_resume.py::test_benchmark_commit_count_is_ceil_per_pass`
- Error: `rebar._store.event_append.StoreError: Error: git commit failed while holding lock: error: unable to create temporary file: Invalid argument`
- Cleared on `recheck` both times → intermittent, not deterministic.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: the B4 batch benchmark test passes on all platforms.
- Actual: intermittent macOS-only failure at the git-commit/tempfile step under the batched-commit path. Likely a macOS CI-runner TMPDIR/tempfile flake, but could be a macOS-specific race in `batch_stage_and_commit`'s tempfile handling worth confirming.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Determine whether the failure is a pure CI-runner tempfile flake or a real macOS-specific issue in `batch_stage_and_commit` / `_recover_from_unmerged` tempfile handling (`src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`).
- [ ] If environmental: harden the test (retry/TMPDIR pin) or mark it appropriately so it does not intermittently red-light unrelated changes.
- [ ] If a real race: fix the tempfile creation under the batch path.
- [ ] `tests/interfaces/io/test_import_batch_resume.py` passes reliably on macOS CI.
compiled_state.title: Flaky macOS CI: test_import_batch_resume tempfile failure under batched commit
